zh.Piercefield Park, Monmouthshire, Wales: 威爾士蒙姆爾皮爾費爾德 帕克
zh.Clungunford, Shropshire, England: 英國史洛普爾可倫根福德
zh.Piercefield, Monmouthshire, Wales: 威爾士蒙姆爾皮爾費爾德 帕克
zh.lifeSummary: 當Anthony William Morris於1557年出生在威爾士蒙姆爾皮爾費爾德 帕克時，他的父親Robert Anthony Morris27歲，母親Elizabeth Field20歲。他於1573年6月21日與Grace Wrighte在英國英格蘭藍卡爾博爾頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1650年在英國威爾斯蒙姆爾享年93歲。

zh-hans.Clungunford, Shropshire, England: 英国史洛普爾可倫根福德
zh-hans.Piercefield, Monmouthshire, Wales: 威尔士蒙姆爾皮爾費爾德 帕克
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Anthony William Morris于1557年出生在威尔士蒙姆爾皮爾費爾德 帕克时，他的父亲Robert Anthony Morris27岁，母亲Elizabeth Field20岁。他于1573年6月21日与Grace Wrighte在英国英格兰藍卡爾博爾頓结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1650年在英国威尔斯蒙姆爾享年93岁。

nameMeanings.name: Morris
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: from Maurice, an Old French personal name introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from Latin Mauritius, a derivative of Maurus (see Moore ). This was the name of several early Christian saints. In some cases it may be a nickname of the same derivation for someone with a swarthy complexion.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Muirghis, a variant of Ó Muirgheasa (see Morrissey ).

Welsh: Anglicized form of the personal name Meurig (from Latin Mauritius), which was gradually superseded in Wales by Morus, Morys, a derivative of the Anglo-Norman French form of the name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Moore
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Mauritz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Armin, Frieda, Helmut, Kurt, Reinhold, Wolfgang.
German and Dutch: from the personal name Mauritz, from Latin Mauritius (see Morris ).

variants.name: Mauricio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Carlos, Mario, Roberto, Lilia, Luis, Adela, Andres, Antero, Cipriano.  Portuguese Manoel.
Portuguese (Maurício) and Spanish: from the personal name Maurício (Portuguese), Mauricio (Spanish), derived from the Latin name Mauritius (see Morris ).

variants.name: Maurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Pierre, Armand, Denys, Gabrielle, Antoine, Benoit, Camille, Cecile, Francois, Gaston, Germain.
French, English, Welsh, Scottish, and Irish: from the Old French personal name Maurice, Latin Mauritius (see Morris ).

variants.name: McMorris
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muiris, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of Morris . In some cases this is a Gaelicized form of Fitzmaurice .
Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muirgheasa ‘son of Muirgheas’, a Gaelic personal name meaning ‘sea valor’ or ‘sea choice’. Compare Morrissey .

variants.name: Morici
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Antonio, Gasper, Aldo, Angelo, Annamarie, Elvio, Giuseppe, Sal, Santo, Vito.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of a variant of the personal name Maurice (see Morris ).
Hungarian: patronymic from the personal name Móric, Hungarian form of Morris .

variants.name: Moris
meanings: English: variant of   Morris  .

French, Luxembourgish, Flemish, Walloon, North German, and Dutch: from a vernacular form of the French personal name Maurice (see Morris ). In North America, the surname Moris may also be an altered form of French Maurice .
Americanized form of German   Moritz  .

variants.name: Merrick
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Meuric, the Middle Welsh form of Maurice (see Morris ).
Irish: borrowed from Welsh. In Ireland, when this is not the Welsh name, it might be adopted for Irish Ó Mearadhaigh, see Merry 2.

variants.name: Maurizio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Marcello, Marco, Mario, Fernando, Matteo, Natale, Sal, Valentino, Vito.
Italian: from the personal name Maurizio, from Latin Mauritius (see Morris 1).

variants.name: Morehouse
meanings: English (Yorkshire): habitational name from any of the many places called Moorhouse in Yorkshire or Durham (from Old English mōr ‘moor’ + hūs ‘house’). In some cases the surname may have become Morris . This name is also established in Ireland.

nameMeanings.name: Anthony
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual English form of the old Roman family name Antonius, which is of uncertain (probably Etruscan) origin. The spelling with -th- (not normally reflected in the pronunciation) represents a learned but erroneous attempt to associate it with Greek anthos ‘flower’. In the post-classical period it was a common name, borne by various early saints, most notably a 3rd-century Egyptian hermit monk, who is regarded as the founder of Christian monasticism.

variants.name: Antony
meanings: Variant spelling of   Anthony .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Anthony William Morris was born in 1557, in Piercefield Park, Monmouthshire, Wales, his father, Robert Anthony Morris, was 27 and his mother, Elizabeth Field, was 20. He married Grace Wrighte on 21 June 1573, in Bolton, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1650, in Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 93.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Anthony William Morris est né en 1557, à Piercefield Park, Monmouthshire, Galles, son père, Robert Anthony Morris, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Field, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Grace Wrighte le 21 juin 1573, à Bolton, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1650, à Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 93 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Anthony William Morris 1557 in Piercefield Park, Monmouthshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Anthony Morris 27 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Field 20. Er hat am 21. Juni 1573 in Bolton, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom Grace Wrighte geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1650 im Alter von 93 Jahren in Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Anthony William Morris è nato il nel 1557 nel Piercefield Park, Monmouthshire, Galles, il suo padre, Robert Anthony Morris, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Field, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Grace Wrighte il 21 giugno 1573, nel Bolton, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1650, nel Monmouthshire, Galles, Regno Unito, all'età di 93 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Anthony William Morris(이)가 1557년 웨일스 몬모우스실르 피어스필드 박에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Anthony Morris(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Field(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1573년 6월 21일 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 볼턴에서 Grace Wrighte(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1650년 영국 웨일스 몬모우스실르에서 93세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Anthony William Morris nasceu em 1557, em Piercefield Park, Monmouthshire, Gales, seu pai, Robert Anthony Morris, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Field, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Grace Wrighte em 21 de junho de 1573, em Bolton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1650, em Monmouthshire, Gales, United Kingdom, com 93 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Anthony William Morris родился в 1557 г. в местности пьирсефиелд парк, монмоутшир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Robert Anthony Morris было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Field, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Grace Wrighte 21 июня 1573 г. в местности болтон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1650 г. в местности монмоутшир, Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 93 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Anthony William Morris nació en 1557, en Piercefield Park, Monmouthshire, Gales, su papá, Robert Anthony Morris, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Field, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Grace Wrighte el 21 de junio de 1573, en Bolton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1650, en Monmouthshire, Gales, Reino Unido, a la edad de 93 años.
